title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation rose from just under $30 billion in 2011 to $80 billion in 2022. The line shows a general upward trajectory over the twelve-year period, beginning with a flat segment between 2011 and 2012 before climbing to roughly $40 billion in 2013. Growth continued steadily to a local peak of approximately $52 billion in 2016, followed by a dip in 2017 and a recovery to roughly $56 billion in 2018. The values remained relatively stable between 2019 and 2020, hovering just above $50 billion, before a steep ascent began in 2021 that carried the total to its highest point on the chart in 2022. The most visible pattern is the acceleration of funding growth in the final two years, where the slope of the line becomes significantly steeper than in previous periods.
